K.N.1.1.desc: Count aloud, forward and backward, to at least 20

K.N.1.2.desc: Recognize without counting (subitize) the quantity of a small group of objects in organized and random arrangements

K.N.1.3.desc: Connect number words and numerals to the quantities they represent

K.N.2.1.desc: Use objects and draw pictures to find the sums and differences of numbers between 0 and 10

K.N.2.2.desc: Demonstrate fluency with addition and subtraction within 5

K.GM.1.1.desc: Recognize and sort basic two-dimensional shapes by attribute

K.GM.1.2.desc: Identify basic three-dimensional shapes in everyday objects

K.GM.2.1.desc: Compare objects by measurable attributes such as length and weight

K.DP.1.1.desc: Collect and organize objects or data into categories and sort the categories by quantity

K.DP.1.2.desc: Create and read simple bar graphs and picture graphs

1.N.1.1.desc: Recognize place value of ones and tens in a two-digit number

1.N.1.2.desc: Use objects and representations to develop understanding of place value

1.N.1.3.desc: Count on from a given number to find the number of objects in a group

1.N.2.1.desc: Use concrete models and drawings to represent addition

1.N.2.2.desc: Demonstrate fluency with addition and subtraction within 10

1.N.2.3.desc: Demonstrate fluency with addition and subtraction within 20

1.A.1.1.desc: Use objects and drawings to represent addition and subtraction situations

1.GM.1.1.desc: Identify and describe two-dimensional shapes and three-dimensional solids

1.GM.2.1.desc: Tell time to the hour using analog and digital clocks

1.DP.1.1.desc: Collect, organize, and represent data in bar graphs and picture graphs

2.N.1.1.desc: Read, write, and represent whole numbers up to 1000

2.N.1.2.desc: Understand place value: ones, tens, and hundreds

2.N.1.3.desc: Use place value understanding to compare numbers

2.N.2.1.desc: Demonstrate fluency with addition and subtraction within 20

2.N.2.2.desc: Use strategies to add and subtract within 100

2.A.1.1.desc: Represent addition and subtraction word problems using equations

2.GM.2.2.desc: Tell time to the nearest 5 minutes

2.DP.1.1.desc: Represent data using bar graphs and line graphs

3.N.1.1.desc: Read, write, and represent fractions with denominators of 2, 3, 4, 6, and 8

3.N.1.2.desc: Represent equivalent fractions using area and length models

3.N.2.1.desc: Represent multiplication and division in various ways

3.N.2.2.desc: Demonstrate fluency with multiplication and division facts within 100

3.N.3.1.desc: Use arrays to model multiplication and division

3.A.1.1.desc: Represent unknown numbers in multiplication and division equations

3.GM.1.3.desc: Identify and describe properties of two-dimensional shapes

3.GM.2.1.desc: Tell time to the nearest minute

3.DP.1.1.desc: Represent data using bar graphs, pictographs, and line plots

4.N.1.1.desc: Demonstrate mastery of multiplication and division basic facts; multiply multi-digit numbers

4.N.1.2.desc: Multiply whole numbers up to four digits by one-digit numbers

4.N.2.1.desc: Represent equivalent fractions using fraction models

4.N.2.3.desc: Add and subtract fractions with like denominators

4.A.1.2.desc: Recognize and describe patterns in addition and multiplication

4.GM.2.1.desc: Measure length and weight using standard units

4.DP.1.1.desc: Represent data using line plots, bar graphs, and tables

5.N.1.1.desc: Divide multi-digit numbers; solve real-world and mathematical problems using arithmetic

5.N.1.2.desc: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators

5.N.2.2.desc: Divide fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by fractions

5.N.3.2.desc: Add and subtract decimals to hundredths

5.A.1.1.desc: Write and interpret expressions using parentheses, brackets, and braces

5.A.1.2.desc: Analyze patterns and relationships in sequences

5.GM.1.2.desc: Understand relationships between area and volume

5.GM.2.1.desc: Graph ordered pairs on the coordinate plane

5.DP.1.1.desc: Represent data using line plots and stem-and-leaf plots

5.DP.1.2.desc: Analyze data using measures of center and spread

6.N.1.1.desc: Represent and compare positive and negative integers using a number line and other models

6.N.1.2.desc: Compare and order positive and negative rational numbers on a number line

6.N.2.1.desc: Determine the greatest common factor and least common multiple of whole numbers

6.N.2.2.desc: Apply the relationship between ratios, equivalent fractions, decimals, and percents

6.N.2.3.desc: Use ratios to represent relationships between quantities in mathematical and real-world contexts

6.N.3.1.desc: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide integers

6.N.3.2.desc: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving fractions, mixed numbers, and rational numbers

6.N.3.3.desc: Calculate percent of a number and solve problems using percent

6.A.1.1.desc: Recognize and describe the connection between operations and their properties

6.A.1.2.desc: Write, read, and evaluate expressions in which letters or symbols stand for numbers

6.A.2.1.desc: Use substitution to determine whether a given number in a specified set makes an equation or inequality true

6.A.2.2.desc: Use variables to represent two quantities in a real-world problem

6.A.3.1.desc: Write and solve equations of the form x+p=q and px=q for cases in which p, q, and x are all nonneg. rational numbers

6.A.3.2.desc: Write an inequality of the form x>c or x<c to represent a constraint or condition

6.GM.1.1.desc: Find the area of polygons by decomposing into triangles and rectangles

6.GM.1.2.desc: Develop and use formulas for the area of triangles, parallelograms, and trapezoids

6.GM.2.1.desc: Find the volume of right rectangular prisms by packing with unit cubes

6.GM.2.2.desc: Represent three-dimensional figures using nets made up of rectangles and triangles

6.GM.3.1.desc: Draw polygons in the coordinate plane given coordinates for the vertices

6.GM.3.2.desc: Find the length of a side joining points with the same first or second coordinate

6.DP.1.1.desc: Display and describe numerical data in plots on a number line, including dot plots, histograms, and box plots

6.DP.1.2.desc: Calculate measures of center (mean, median) and measures of variability (range, IQR, MAD)

6.DP.1.3.desc: Summarize numerical data sets in relation to their context

7.N.1.1.desc: Know that every rational number can be written as a ratio of two integers or as a terminating or repeating decimal

7.N.1.2.desc: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational numbers with fluency

7.N.1.3.desc: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving the four operations with rational numbers

7.A.1.1.desc: Decide whether two quantities are in a proportional relationship and identify the constant of proportionality

7.A.1.2.desc: Represent proportional relationships by equations and explain the meaning of the constant of proportionality

7.A.1.3.desc: Explain what a point on a graph of a proportional relationship means in terms of the situation

7.A.2.1.desc: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add, subtract, factor, and expand linear expressions

7.A.2.2.desc: Understand that rewriting an expression in different forms can shed light on the problem

7.A.3.1.desc: Solve multi-step real-world and mathematical problems posed with positive and negative rational numbers

7.A.3.2.desc: Use variables to represent quantities in a real-world or mathematical problem and construct equations to solve

7.A.4.1.desc: Solve word problems leading to inequalities of the form px+q>r or px+q<r

7.GM.1.1.desc: Solve problems involving scale drawings of geometric figures

7.GM.1.2.desc: Know the formulas for the area and circumference of a circle; use them to solve problems

7.GM.2.1.desc: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving area, volume, and surface area of objects

7.GM.2.2.desc: Describe the two-dimensional figures that result from slicing three-dimensional figures

7.DP.1.1.desc: Understand that statistics can be used to gain information about a population by examining a sample

7.DP.1.2.desc: Use data from a random sample to draw inferences about a population with an unknown characteristic

7.DP.2.1.desc: Understand that the probability of a chance event is a number between 0 and 1

7.DP.2.2.desc: Develop a probability model and use it to find probabilities of events

7.DP.2.3.desc: Find probabilities of compound events using organized lists, tables, tree diagrams, and simulation

8.N.1.1.desc: Know that numbers that are not rational are called irrational; know that every number has a decimal expansion

8.N.1.2.desc: Use rational approximations of irrational numbers to compare the size of irrational numbers

8.N.1.3.desc: Express very large and very small quantities in scientific notation and interpret in context

8.A.1.1.desc: Graph proportional relationships, interpreting the unit rate as the slope of the graph; compare two different proportional relationships

8.A.1.2.desc: Use similar triangles to explain why the slope m is the same between any two points on a non-vertical line

8.A.1.3.desc: Derive the equation y=mx+b for a line; interpret the meaning of slope and y-intercept

8.A.2.1.desc: Solve linear equations in one variable with rational coefficients

8.A.2.2.desc: Solve pairs of simultaneous linear equations using substitution and elimination

8.A.3.1.desc: Understand that a function is a rule that assigns exactly one output to each input

8.A.3.2.desc: Compare properties of two functions each represented in a different way

8.A.3.3.desc: Interpret the equation y=mx+b as defining a linear function; give examples of non-linear functions

8.GM.1.1.desc: Verify experimentally the properties of rotations, reflections, and translations

8.GM.1.2.desc: Understand that a two-dimensional figure is congruent to another if it can be obtained by a sequence of rotations, reflections, and translations

8.GM.1.3.desc: Describe the effect of dilations, translations, rotations, and reflections on two-dimensional figures

8.GM.1.4.desc: Understand that a two-dimensional figure is similar to another if it can be obtained by dilations, translations, rotations, and reflections

8.GM.2.1.desc: Explain a proof of the Pythagorean theorem and its converse

8.GM.2.2.desc: Apply the Pythagorean theorem to determine unknown side lengths in right triangles

8.GM.2.3.desc: Apply the Pythagorean theorem to find the distance between two points in a coordinate system

8.GM.3.1.desc: Know and apply the formulas for the volumes of cones, cylinders, and spheres

8.DP.1.1.desc: Construct and interpret scatter plots for bivariate measurement data to investigate patterns of association

8.DP.1.2.desc: Know that straight lines are widely used to model relationships between two quantitative variables; assess the model

8.DP.1.3.desc: Use the equation of a linear model to solve problems in the context of bivariate measurement data
